quickSummary: Weekend edition: Boredom Baron's European Fear Gauge hit 82/100 (PANIC) — a 38-point weekly surge — as the Strait of Hormuz closure created the largest energy supply disruption since 2022. Coughlin Cap argues Coupang's -40% drawdown is overblown. Made In Japan examines AI disruption of Japanese software. Nate's blind eval finds GPT-5.4 fails common sense but excels at quant tasks. NFP -92K, oil $90, Fear & Greed at 27.

keyThemes.title: Hormuz Closure / PANIC
keyThemes.description: Boredom Baron European Fear Gauge surged to 82/100 (PANIC) from 44. VSTOXX went from 18.3 to 90 in one week. Largest energy supply disruption since Ukraine invasion.

keyThemes.title: Dollar is King
keyThemes.description: Gold declined despite a live shooting war. Capital wants 'dollars, and only dollars.' Safe-haven demand jumped 11x but flows to USD, not gold.

keyThemes.title: CPNG Breach Overblown
keyThemes.description: Coughlin Cap: Revenue $8.8B (+14% CC), breach forensics show no financial data compromised. Stock -40% from highs, near lowest-ever multiples.

keyThemes.title: GPT-5.4 vs Claude vs Gemini
keyThemes.description: Nate's blind eval: GPT-5.4 fails basic common sense but wins on quant modeling. Models converging on capability, diverging on philosophy.

keyThemes.title: Stagflation Risk Rising
keyThemes.description: NFP -92K jobs, unemployment 4.4%, oil at $90, mortgage rates at 6%. Prolonged Hormuz closure + weak labor market = stagflationary setup.

fearGreedIndex.label: Fear
fearGreedIndex.context: Down 6 points to 27, approaching Extreme Fear. Dropped 19 points in two weeks (46 to 27). Dow worst week since April. Oil $90. NFP -92K. 1 week ago: 38. 1 month ago: 46. 1 year ago: 17 (Extreme Fear).

stockHighlights.context: Q4 rev $8.8B (+14% CC), breach overblown per Coughlin Cap, stock -40% from highs
CEO Jeff Green $148M purchase (+373% position) — dominant insider signal of the week
4-person cluster buy totaling $15.6M from both Co-CEOs + directors
McCoy Global -18% after Q4/2025 annual earnings, Wolf of Oakville reviewing thesis
$90/bbl, biggest weekly surge since 2022, Hormuz effectively closed
Made In Japan: structural buffers but transition pain ahead from AI disruption

high: Oil/Energy — $90 oil, Hormuz closure dominating macro discussion

insiderBuysNote: Top 20 open-market purchases from SEC Form 4 filings (Feb 27 – Mar 7, 2026).
insiderBuysSignals: TTD CEO Jeff Green's $148M purchase (+373% position) remains the dominant signal. KKR saw a 4-person cluster buy totaling $15.6M from both Co-CEOs + directors. Eric Sprott doubled down on HYMC with two consecutive purchases ($8.8M). NMFC's Steven Klinsky made 3 separate purchases totaling ~$8M. Breadth of insider buying during a panic week is a historically bullish contrarian signal.

macroOverview.author: Darius Dale / Boredom Baron
macroOverview.title: Stagflation risk rises as Hormuz closure meets labor market deterioration

themes.title: Energy
themes.description: Oil at $90, biggest weekly surge since 2022. Hormuz effectively closed. Saudi Arabia hiking prices. U.S. SPR depleted.

themes.title: Labor Market
themes.description: NFP -92K jobs, unemployment 4.4%. Worst jobs report since 2021. Stagflation risk rising.

themes.title: European Panic
themes.description: Boredom Baron Fear Gauge 82/100 (PANIC). VSTOXX 18.3 to 90 in one week. Private credit redemptions. IPO market frozen.

themes.title: Dollar / Safe Haven
themes.description: USD sole safe haven. Gold declining despite shooting war. Market says 'I want dollars, and only dollars.'

themes.title: Asia
themes.description: Morgan Stanley downgraded entire Asian region. India secured 30-day Russian crude waiver but can't offset 2.6M bpd ME exposure.

themes.title: Insider Buying
themes.description: $350M+ in open-market purchases this week. TTD $148M, KKR $15.6M cluster. Historically bullish contrarian signal.

posts.title: Weekly Intelligence Briefing (6 March 2026)
posts.summary: European Fear Gauge surged to 82/100 (PANIC) from 44. VSTOXX 18.3 to 90 in one week. Hormuz effectively closed — largest energy supply disruption since Ukraine invasion. Dollar is king — gold declining despite war. Safe-haven demand jumped 11x. Geopolitical risk score: -8 (deepest negative). Panics are 'the best thing that ever happens to watchlists.'

posts.title: Coupang (CPNG): Q4 2025 Earnings Review
posts.summary: Revenue $8.8B (+14% CC). Core Korean Product Commerce growing 16% CC before December data breach. January was trough (~4% CC), February improving. Mandiant/Palo Alto forensics: no financial data compromised. Stock -40% from highs, near lowest-ever multiples. Author bullish: breach narrative overblown.

posts.title: McCoy Global ($MCB.TO) FINS Review
posts.summary: MCB dropped 18% after Q4/2025 annual earnings. Author's 'Seal of Approval' pick at $2.39 had appreciated 85% before this drop, now only up 12%. Had flagged as 'danger zone' in February but had reasons wrong. Paywalled deep dive. Canadian oilfield services microcap.

posts.title: GPT-5.4 beat human performance on desktop tasks and missed a question a child would get right
posts.summary: Blind eval of GPT-5.4 vs Claude Opus 4.6 vs Gemini 3.1. GPT-5.4 failed trivial common-sense question but wins on quant modeling and file processing. 'Toggle' splits model into two products. 'Pipeline problem' failure mode. Models converging on capability, diverging on philosophy. OpenAI building agentic infrastructure.

posts.title: Japanese Software will be okay: Thoughts (and prayers)
posts.summary: AI (Claude) impact on Japanese software: business model disruption (shorter dev cycles, more auditing) and lower switching costs (COBOL modernization). But Japan has structural buffers: tight labor market, cultural enterprise relationships, regulatory complexity. Selloff partly justified but Japanese software more resilient than global narrative suggests.
